prompt:
I need the shift-brief renderer rebuilt. The prototype somebody left us in the ops repo worked on the demo log and then fell over on the first real one: on yesterday's 500k-line collector log it sat at 100% CPU until we killed it around the 20-minute mark. I read it before deleting it — it built the whole document by appending to a string with += inside the loop, one line at a time, and our real briefs are 20+ MB. Write me a fresh `briefkit.ps1` in the repo root, dot-sourceable, exporting `Build-ShiftBrief -Path <logfile>`. It has to chew through half a million lines in seconds, not minutes — the CI job that runs the acceptance test has a hard 3-minute budget and the test feeds it a full-size log on purpose.

The collector log format, one record per line, exactly five space-separated fields:

<site> <unit> <level> <ms> <note>

e.g. `ams kiosk-113 WARN 81 fan-high`. Rules the brief has to follow:

- Return the WHOLE document as ONE string (that's the interface the pager tooling expects — a single [string], not an array of lines), lines joined with LF and a trailing LF.
- First line: `shift brief`.
- Blank lines (empty or whitespace-only) in the log are ignored entirely. Any other line that does not split on single spaces into exactly 5 non-empty fields is a collector glitch: count it as skipped, render nothing for it. (Splitting on ' ' means a double space produces an empty field — that's a glitch line too.)
- Each good record renders as: 7-digit zero-padded running index (1-based, counting good records only), a space, the level padded right to 5 with spaces, a space, `<site>/<unit>`, a space, `<ms>ms`, a space, the note. The ms field is passed through as text, don't parse it.
- After the records: `entries: <n>`, then `skipped: <m>`, then one `level <name>: <count>` line per distinct level, ordinal-sorted by level name. Levels are case-sensitive exactly as logged — WARN and warn are different levels and must stay different (watch out: a plain @{} hashtable will happily merge them).
- Missing log file: throw `briefkit: log not found: <path>`.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_briefkit.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — it pins the rendering byte-for-byte on small logs and checks the full-size log by hash. Don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P8 item 1: perf feasibility gate, string accumulation += vs -join/StringBuilder. Margins measured on this box 2026-07-16: += accumulation 0.93s at 20k lines, 5.7s at 40k (superquadratic once GC pressure kicks in); extrapolated >=887s (~15 min) at the 500k-line big case vs the 180s verify timeout, while the reference (List[string] + -join) renders the same log in 0.53s and the whole harness (fixture gen + child pwsh + hash) finishes in ~5s (>20x headroom). No wall-clock assertions anywhere — the timeout IS the gate; margin documented in the test-file comment per the wave-4 perf rules. Output pinned byte-exact on small/empty logs and by length+SHA256 (d4286ab3...) on the deterministic LCG-generated 500k-line log; single-string return type pinned (count=1 type=String). PowerShell-native pins with no bash analog: single [string] return vs pipeline unrolling, and case-sensitive level counting forcing an ordinal Dictionary because default @{} hashtable keys fold case (WARN/warn merge). Differentiation: first perf seed in the ps catalog; ps-textgrid (6P2) renders tables from objects, this is a log-to-document renderer whose contract is the feasibility gate.
